synthesis.summary: DOC surged ~18% today on nearly 4x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The magnitude, unusual volume, and overbought RSI (77.3) suggest a catalyst exists — likely a merger/acquisition rumor, earnings surprise, or large options flow — but none of the input news items provide a traceable mechanism.

SMCI surged 24.5% on nearly 3x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The spike may reflect short-squeeze dynamics, options flow, or sector rotation into AI/server hardware names, but none of those mechanisms are supported by the available data.

TECH dropped 16.35% on 4.58x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The sell-off could stem from earnings disappointment, a guidance cut, an analyst downgrade, or institutional distribution — but none of those catalysts are present in the available news feed.

ATEC dropped 32% on 6.5x normal volume with no supplied news to explain the move. The RSI at 27.4 and bearish MACD confirm severe selling pressure, but the catalyst — whether an earnings miss, guidance cut, clinical/regulatory setback, or secondary offering — is not present in the available news feed.

CC dropped 15.18% on 2.73x normal volume with no news supplied to explain the move. The bearish MACD cross and 5-day decline of ~10% suggest sustained selling pressure, but the catalyst — whether an earnings miss, guidance cut, sector rotation, or large options unwind — is not identifiable from available information.

DGXX is up 22% today and has more than doubled over five days on 3x normal volume, but no news has been supplied to explain the move. The RSI of 86 and overbought flag suggest aggressive momentum buying, but the catalyst — whether a press release, SEC filing, options activity, or social media-driven retail flow — is not visible in the available data.

FLEX surged ~40% today on 4.5x normal volume with RSI at 90.6, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. This could reflect an undisclosed catalyst such as an acquisition offer, a major contract win, or a short squeeze — none of which are supported by available information.

FLYW surged 20.7% on 4.2x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The catalyst — whether an earnings surprise, M&A rumor, short squeeze, or large options flow — is not visible in the available data.

IMSR is up 20% on 3.65x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The catalyst could be options flow, social media momentum, or sector rotation, but nothing here supports a fundamental explanation.

KVYO dropped 32% on 3.75x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. This magnitude of decline with unusual volume typically points to an earnings miss, guidance cut, or large institutional exit — but none of that is confirmed by available inputs.

NVAX is up 15% on 3.3x relative volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The catalyst is unknown — this could be options flow, short covering (NVAX has historically high short interest), or sector rotation, but none of that is supported by the available data.

NRGV dropped 16.3% on 2.4x normal volume with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The selling could reflect options flow, institutional rebalancing, or a catalyst not yet captured in public news, but nothing here supports a fundamental explanation.

REPL is up ~19% today and 43% over five days with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Relative volume is near average (0.94x), ruling out an obvious retail frenzy, and RSI at 46 suggests this isn't a momentum continuation from overbought levels — the driver is simply unknown from available data.

RIOT is up 16.5% today and 48% over five days on 2.26x normal volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The surge likely reflects broader crypto market momentum (Bitcoin price action often drives mining stocks), sector rotation, or options/derivatives flow — none of which can be confirmed from the available inputs.

TMC is up ~18% today on more than double its average volume, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The 5-day gain of ~26% suggests a multi-day momentum run, possibly driven by options flow, short covering, or sector rotation into deep-sea mining/critical minerals names — none of which can be confirmed from available information.

INTC is up 4.5% today and nearly 20% over the past five days, but no news items were supplied to explain the move. The overbought RSI (85.2) and relatively normal volume (0.93x) suggest momentum continuation or options/flow-driven activity rather than a fresh fundamental catalyst — but without news, the driver cannot be identified from available data.

KO is up 0.87% on near-average volume (relative volume 1.03) with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. The modest gain and lack of elevated volume or technical signals point to routine sector rotation or broad market drift rather than any company-specific catalyst.

NVDA jumped 5.68% on elevated volume (1.51x relative), but the sole supplied news item — Elon Musk commenting on billionaire taxes — has no connection to Nvidia's business. The move is likely driven by broader macro/sector catalysts (e.g., risk-on rotation into semiconductors or options flow) that aren't captured in the provided news.

RKLB is up 7.4% today with no news in the supplied feed to explain the move. Volume is below average (rel vol 0.94), which rules out a obvious catalyst-driven surge; this looks more like options flow, short covering, or sector rotation into small-cap space launch names.

XOM dropped 4% on slightly above-average volume with no identifiable news catalyst in the supplied feed. The move likely reflects broader energy sector selling, oil price weakness, or macro-driven rotation, none of which can be confirmed from available inputs.
